hero.name: Mohamed Ahmed Mohamed Naji Muthana
hero.subtitle: I build useful web products, e-commerce systems, and business tools with a security-aware mindset. Based in Sana'a, Yemen.

about.title: About Me
about.p1: I hold a Bachelor's degree in Cybersecurity from Al-Rasheed Smart University. I have hands-on experience building and connecting digital platforms, including e-commerce and inventory systems.
about.p2: My interests span UI/UX, frontend development, cybersecurity, and overarching digital systems. I am currently open to opportunities in Yemen or remote roles where I can combine my design sensibilities with robust, secure technical implementations.

jouda-food.title: Jouda Food
jouda-food.problem: Users needed a way to reliably identify gluten-free products and shop from a curated food store without confusion.
jouda-food.goal: Build an accessible, user-friendly e-commerce platform dedicated to gluten-free foods, ensuring clear product categorization and smooth checkout.
jouda-food.role: Full-stack Developer & Platform Manager. I built and managed the platform, designed customer-facing interfaces, organized products and workflows, connected the store with an inventory management system, and helped deploy and maintain the platform.
jouda-food.solution: Developed a modern storefront tailored for gluten-free product discovery. Integrated seamless navigation, robust search filtering, and clear product labeling.
jouda-food.challenges: Synchronizing the online catalog with the physical inventory in real-time to avoid selling out-of-stock items, and designing a clear classification system for varying dietary restrictions.
jouda-food.outcome: Successfully launched the platform, providing a reliable shopping hub for customers with specific dietary needs, supported by a stable and synchronized backend.
jouda-food.lessons: Learned the importance of deep integration between e-commerce frontends and inventory backends, and how vital clear UI communication is for niche markets.

inventory-system.title: Multi-Branch Inventory Management System
inventory-system.problem: Managing inventory, products, and stock operations across multiple business branches was highly manual, leading to discrepancies between physical stock and the online store.
inventory-system.goal: Design and implement a centralized inventory management system capable of supporting multiple branches and syncing seamlessly with an e-commerce platform.
inventory-system.role: System Designer & Integrator. I designed inventory workflows, connected the system with the e-commerce platform, supported database integration, and organized stock and product management processes.
inventory-system.solution: Created a centralized dashboard that tracks stock levels across all physical branches and automatically reflects these numbers on the online storefront.
inventory-system.challenges: Handling race conditions during high-volume sales where both physical and online purchases occur simultaneously.
inventory-system.outcome: Streamlined inventory workflows, significantly reducing out-of-stock errors and administrative overhead for branch managers.
inventory-system.lessons: Gained practical experience in designing robust database schemas for complex business operations and handling cross-system synchronization.

malware-analysis.title: Windows Malware Analysis System
malware-analysis.problem: Relying on a single method of malware detection often leaves gaps, allowing sophisticated threats to bypass security measures.
malware-analysis.goal: Develop an integrated cybersecurity project for analyzing suspicious Windows files through multiple detection methodologies.
malware-analysis.role: System Architect & Security Analyst. I designed the overall system workflow, integrated multiple analysis approaches, and worked on malware detection and threat assessment concepts.
malware-analysis.solution: Built a comprehensive analysis pipeline that subjects suspicious files to static, dynamic, and machine learning-based assessments, cross-referenced with threat intelligence feeds.
malware-analysis.challenges: Safely executing and monitoring dynamic analysis without exposing the host system, and tuning the ML models to reduce false positives.
malware-analysis.outcome: Created a robust, multi-layered detection system capable of identifying complex malware that would otherwise evade basic signature-based scanners.
malware-analysis.lessons: Learned how different analysis techniques complement each other, and gained deep insights into Windows internals and malware evasion tactics.
